nodes.name: Add Law Collection and Loader
nodes.rationale: System needed to ingest and search Social Insurance Law of the PRC, which has a chapter-article structure different from FAQ markdown. Two VectorStoreManager instances sharing the same Milvus uri/token is fine - overhead is one extra HTTP connection at most. Merge before rerank because cross-encoder is the bottleneck.

nodes.name: Add BM25 Hybrid Search
nodes.rationale: Insurance terminology (clause numbers, legal citations, domain jargon) requires exact keyword matching that dense vectors alone cannot provide. BM25 sparse search naturally excels at tf-idf matching for rare terms, complementing dense semantic search.

nodes.name: LawLoader
nodes.rationale: Splits law markdown on ## for chapters filtering out TOC and source line, then regex-splits on **第X条** (Chinese numerals) for articles within each chapter. Builds chapter-prefixed headers like '工伤保险 - 第三十六条' for self-documenting retrieved results.

nodes.name: BM25 Sparse Vector
nodes.rationale: Milvus built-in BM25EmbeddingFunction auto-generates SPARSE_FLOAT_VECTOR from text field on insert/upsert. Zero extra dependencies, zero network calls, fully server-side.

nodes.name: RRF Fusion Strategy
nodes.rationale: RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) chosen over WeightedRanker because BM25 scores and cosine similarity operate on different scales; RRF is rank-based and naturally scale-agnostic, working out of the box without parameter tuning.

nodes.name: Three-Route Hybrid Search
nodes.rationale: Three routes (content_vector + header_vector + sparse_vector) because header and content occupy different semantic spaces (question heading vs answer body). RRF fuses three routes with no added complexity.

nodes.name: Dual-Language Query Rewrite
nodes.rationale: QueryRewriteNode returns separate rewritten queries for zh-CN and en-US. Each query is optimized for its target language's vocabulary and BM25 tokenization. Deduplication by id prevents the same FAQ section appearing twice if retrieved by both language searches.

nodes.name: Multi-Collection Retrieval
nodes.rationale: RetrievalNode iterates all VectorStoreManager instances and merges results before deduplication and reranking. Merging first and reranking once is more efficient than reranking per-collection. Two collections is not enough to justify a factory or registry pattern.

nodes.name: Chapter-Article Parsing
nodes.rationale: Article-level chunks because articles are self-contained legal provisions (one provision = one rule). Chapter-level chunks would be too large for precise retrieval. Chapter prefix in header because an article number like '第三十六条' is meaningless without knowing it belongs to the Work Injury Insurance chapter.

nodes.name: Multi-Level Ingestion
nodes.rationale: Full article stored as one record plus chunked segments when article exceeds 200 chars. Segments use RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter (chunk_size=300, chunk_overlap=20) with chapter-prefixed heading prepended, marked with is_segment=True.

nodes.name: Idempotent MD5 Loading
nodes.rationale: MD5-based deterministic IDs for upsert. Re-running the law loader upserts records rather than creating duplicates.

nodes.name: Thread ID Memory
nodes.rationale: thread_id extracted from request or generated as UUID when missing. Enables InMemorySaver checkpointing per conversation thread for multi-turn memory persistence. Stored in sessionStorage on the frontend.

nodes.name: Milvus BM25 Function
nodes.rationale: Built-in BM25EmbeddingFunction defined in collection schema. Milvus auto-computes sparse_vector from the text field on insert/upsert with enable_analyzer=True for BM25 tokenization.

nodes.name: Fix GraphProcessor Memory — Design Document
nodes.description: Design decisions for fixing WorkflowState add_messages reducer bug and adding thread_id session isolation in GraphProcessor

nodes.description: Proposal describing multi-turn memory fix, thread_id support, and frontend sessionStorage changes

nodes.name: Refactor Graph Processor Split — Design Document
nodes.description: Design for extracting GraphProcessor nodes into standalone classes with pipeline-stage package layout, standard node contract, and dependency injection

nodes.name: Refactor Graph Processor Split — Proposal
nodes.description: Proposal introducing graph-node-architecture capability for standardized LangGraph node design

nodes.name: Graph Node Architecture Spec
nodes.description: Specification of standard node contract, pipeline-stage sub-packages, dependency injection pattern, structured output placement, and third-party runnable usage

nodes.name: Refactor Graph Processor Split — Tasks
nodes.description: Task breakdown for extracting state, creating node sub-packages, rewriting GraphProcessor, and updating imports

nodes.name: Prompt Registry — Design Document

nodes.name: Prompt Registry — Proposal

nodes.name: Prompt Registry Spec
nodes.description: Spec requiring typed prompt discovery, access, CRISPE compliance, and Jinja2 variable injection

nodes.name: Prompt Templating Spec

nodes.name: Configurable AI Models — Tasks
nodes.description: Task breakdown for config.toml creation, ConfigManager implementation, ChatProcessor refactoring, and tests

nodes.name: WorkflowState add_messages reducer bug
nodes.description: Field(default_factory=list) inside Annotated block causes LangGraph to ignore add_messages reducer, overwriting message state instead of appending
nodes.rationale: Move Field definition outside Annotated block so LangGraph/Pydantic recognizes the reducer (Decision 1 of fix-graphprocessor-memory design). The annotation order is required by LangGraph for the reducer to be recognized.

nodes.name: thread_id session isolation
nodes.description: Server-managed thread_id for isolating user conversational sessions; optional in request, server generates UUID if absent, returned in response JSON
nodes.rationale: Enables browser to manage sessions via sessionStorage without client-side UUID generation (Decision 2 of fix-graphprocessor-memory design). If provided the server uses it to retrieve existing state; if missing the server generates a UUIDv4.

nodes.name: Frontend sessionStorage thread_id management
nodes.description: Frontend uses sessionStorage.getItem and sessionStorage.setItem for thread_id persistence across the page session
nodes.rationale: sessionStorage persists for the duration of the page session (tab), matching multi-turn conversation requirements (Decision 3 of fix-graphprocessor-memory design).

nodes.name: ConfigManager class
nodes.description: TOML-based centralized configuration utility that handles load_dotenv() and provides AI model settings via structured accessors
nodes.rationale: Encapsulates configuration logic and provides a single source of truth. TOML chosen over .env for readability with complex model parameters (Decision 1-2 of configurable-ai-models design). Uses Python 3.11+ stdlib tomllib.

nodes.name: init_chat_model factory function
nodes.description: LangChain factory function for standardized model initialization, enabling provider switching via configuration
nodes.rationale: Simplifies model instantiation and makes provider switching (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Anthropic) config-driven rather than hardcoded (Decision 3 of configurable-ai-models design).

nodes.name: TOML configuration for AI models
nodes.description: config.toml defines AI model parameters (provider, model name, temperature) parsed by ConfigManager

nodes.name: PromptRegistry class
nodes.description: Centralized registry managing prompts as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, organized by type in subfolders, supporting Jinja2 templating and lazy indexing

nodes.name: CRISPE prompt engineering principle
nodes.description: Capacity, Insight, Statement, Personality, Experiment — standard for structuring system prompts to ensure high-quality AI behavior

nodes.name: Jinja2 templating for prompts
nodes.description: Jinja2 for variable injection (loops, conditionals, filters) within Markdown prompt templates using variables dict
nodes.rationale: Industry standard for Python templating, already widely used in LangChain ecosystems, superior to f-strings which lack loops and conditionals (Decision 3 of prompt-registry design).

nodes.name: Lazy indexing in PromptRegistry
nodes.description: PromptRegistry scans directory on init to map names but only loads and parses content on demand, keeping startup fast
nodes.rationale: Keeps startup fast by deferring file content loading until first access (Decision 4 of prompt-registry design).

nodes.name: YAML frontmatter parsing for prompts
nodes.description: Extracts YAML metadata (type, description, variables) from Markdown files delimited by ---, determines prompt type from subfolder rather than frontmatter

nodes.name: Standard LangGraph node contract (state, config) -> dict
nodes.description: Every graph node is a class with __init__ receiving dependencies and __call__(self, state: WorkflowState, config: RunnableConfig) -> dict for state transformation
nodes.rationale: LangGraph natively invokes nodes as node(state, config). Accepting config enables future access to configurable fields like thread_id. Parameter is optional with default for nodes that don't need it (Decision 2 of refactor-split design).

nodes.name: Dependency injection at GraphProcessor composition root
nodes.description: GraphProcessor.__init__ reads cfg and injects dependencies into node constructors; nodes never read global cfg directly
nodes.rationale: Keeps nodes decoupled from global config and makes them testable with fakes (Decision 6 of refactor-split design). Config values threaded through constructors from GraphProcessor.__init__.

nodes.name: Pipeline-stage sub-package layout
nodes.description: Graph nodes grouped by pipeline stage: guard/ (intent_check, unhandled), memory/ (summarize), search/ (query_rewrite, retrieval), response/ (inquiry)
nodes.rationale: Grouping by stage makes the pipeline's structure visible in the filesystem and keeps related nodes co-located. Rejected flat nodes/ directory approach (Decision 1 of refactor-split design).

nodes.name: Raw SummarizationNode without adapter wrapper
nodes.description: langmem's SummarizationNode registered directly in graph assembly due to compatible Runnable signature, no adapter class needed
nodes.rationale: SummarizationNode already conforms to LangGraph's invocation protocol. Wrapping would add indirection without benefit (Decision 3 of refactor-split design).

nodes.name: Structured output models co-located with nodes
nodes.description: Pydantic models (IntentCheckResult, InquiryResult, QueryRewriteResult) defined in same module as the node that uses them
nodes.rationale: These models are implementation details of how a specific node calls with_structured_output — they don't need to be shared (Decision 5 of refactor-split design).

nodes.name: Spec-driven change workflow (openspec)
nodes.description: All changes follow openspec schema with .openspec.yaml metadata, proposal.md, design.md, and specs/ artifacts

attributes.rationale: Refactor Flask startup to use application factory pattern, add ModelRegistry, blueprint modularization, health check endpoint, and graceful warm-up sequence
Standard Flask best practice that allows creating multiple app instances with different configs and prevents side effects during import
Centralized registry for managing LLM model instances, provider settings, and aliases with lazy instantiation
Organize routing logic into dedicated blueprints package (chat, monitoring) with registration helper
Health check endpoint that returns 200 with healthy status or 503 with error details, decoupling app liveness from external provider availability
Structured startup process that verifies AI model connectivity and internal state before signaling readiness
Add formal CLI entrypoint via Poetry scripts to enable poetry run muggle as primary startup command
Encapsulated application startup logic in muggle/app.py serving as Poetry script entrypoint
Remove redundant is_registered check from warm_up method by delegating model existence validation to ModelRegistry.get_model()
Standardize node/prompt naming to nouns, make max_attempts configurable, replace validation_router function with ValidationRouter class
Callable class replacing validation_router function, receives max_attempts at construction instead of hardcoding, routes to END/FallbackNode/Summarization based on validation state
Renamed from UnhandledNode. 'Fallback' describes what it IS (fallback path), consistent with noun naming convention
Renamed from ValidateNode to follow consistent noun naming convention across all graph nodes
Renamed from retry_count; 'attempt' accurately counts all validation rounds including the first, not just retries
Configurable via [validate] section in config.toml instead of hardcoded to 5; default 5 if not specified
Add LLM-as-judge retrieval evaluation script using Ragas metrics to scale evaluation beyond human-labeled relevant_ids
Standalone evaluation script using Ragas Context Precision, Context Recall, Context Relevance, and Context Entity Recall metrics
Four Ragas metrics: ContextPrecision (LLM, needs reference), ContextRecall (LLM, needs reference), ContextRelevance (LLM, no reference needed), ContextEntityRecall (lexical, needs reference)
Ragas data structure containing user_input (query), retrieved_contexts (retrieved docs), and reference (ground truth)
Evaluates vector-only (dense ANN), hybrid (dense + sparse BM25 with RRF), and hybrid+rerank (with DashScope rerank) configurations side by side
Existing label-driven retrieval eval using P@k, R@k, nDCG, MRR requiring human-labeled relevant_ids

properties.name: Application Factory Pattern
properties.rationale: Standard Flask best practice. Allows creating multiple app instances with different configs (e.g., for testing). Prevents side effects during import.

properties.name: Flask Blueprints Package
properties.rationale: Decouples routing logic from the main application entry point. Package structure makes the project easier to navigate and scale.

properties.name: Model Registry Pattern
properties.rationale: Centralizes model management. Allows supporting multiple models, handling aliasing, and provides a clean interface for ChatProcessor and other components.

properties.name: Health Check Endpoint
properties.rationale: Decouples application liveness from external provider availability. Confirms server is correctly configured and ready to handle requests.

properties.name: Graceful Warm-up
properties.rationale: Ensures internal state and connectivity are established before signaling readiness. Can start in degraded state if warm-up times out.

properties.name: Chat API
properties.rationale: Backend exposes POST /chat endpoint within a dedicated chat blueprint for user message processing.

properties.name: Component Initialization
properties.rationale: Move ChatProcessor initialization into setup_components(app) called by create_app to ensure dependencies are initialized within application context.

properties.name: Multi-Node RAG Pipeline
properties.rationale: Integrate VectorStoreManager into GraphProcessor for grounded responses via Rewrite -> Retrieve -> Inquire workflow.

properties.name: Query Rewrite Node
properties.rationale: Decoupling query optimization from response generation allows better tuning of retrieval accuracy.

properties.name: Retrieval Node
properties.rationale: Pure Python/IO node that does not require an LLM call, reducing costs and latency for that specific step.

properties.name: Inquiry Node
properties.rationale: Augments prompt with retrieved context and generates grounded response using Jinja2 templating.

properties.name: State Separation
properties.rationale: Use dedicated fields (vector_store_query, context) instead of modifying messages to keep conversation history clean.

properties.name: Jinja2 Prompt Templating
properties.rationale: PromptRegistry is already configured with Jinja2, allowing clean template separation and conditional logic via {{ context }} placeholder.

properties.name: Top-K Retrieval Configuration
properties.rationale: Allows easy tuning of retrieval depth without code changes, balancing context richness vs. token cost.

properties.name: Dependency Injection
properties.rationale: Pass VectorStoreManager to GraphProcessor.__init__ for cleanest way to provide required infrastructure components.

properties.name: Context-Aware Query Rewriting
properties.rationale: Rewrite conversational queries like follow-ups into standalone search queries, stored in vector_store_query state field.

properties.name: FAQ Similarity Search
properties.rationale: Return top N relevant FAQ entries from Milvus using configured embedding model, formatted with separators between header and text.

properties.name: Structured Node Execution
properties.rationale: Standard LangChain way to enforce structured responses without agent loop overhead. Uses with_structured_output natively with Pydantic.

properties.name: Explicit Message Preparation
properties.rationale: Each node constructs message list (SystemMessage + state.messages) for full visibility into conversation history passed to LLM.

properties.name: AgentExecutor Elimination
properties.rationale: Eliminates agent-loop overhead, reduces latency, and removes risk of implicit tool-calling hallucinations.

properties.name: Unified Error Handling
properties.rationale: Nodes handle parsing errors or LLM failures consistently, potentially falling back to safe defaults if structured output fails.

properties.name: Warm-up Removal
properties.rationale: Warm-up is redundant with constructor. Constructor already calls registry.get_model(default_model). Failing at first request is equivalent behavior with less machinery.

properties.name: Health Monitoring Simplification
properties.rationale: is_initialized() tracked only warm-up success which equals constructor success. Equivalent to processor existence check.

properties.name: ProcessorInterface Stability
properties.rationale: ProcessorInterface ABC remains unchanged during warm-up removal; only GraphProcessor implementation is affected.

properties.name: src-layout-with-layers
properties.rationale: Move to src/ layout with layered architecture (core, infra, app, shared) for modularity and to prevent circular dependencies. Uses importlib.resources for prompt management.

properties.name: ChatProcessor
properties.rationale: Modular processor interface decoupling Flask routes from AI implementation. Accepts message, returns response. Designed to be swappable with LangGraph workflow later.

properties.name: Flask Chat API
properties.rationale: POST /chat endpoint accepting JSON {message} and returning {response}. Lightweight backend using Flask.

properties.name: DeepSeek via LangChain
properties.rationale: Primary LLM model used via LangChain abstraction for Q&A tasks.

properties.name: ValidateNode
properties.rationale: LLM-based quality gate scoring InquiryNode output against prompt-validate.md rubric. Uses structured output with ValidationResult Pydantic model (decision, score, critical_flaws). Configurable threshold from config.toml.

properties.name: Validation Retry Loop
properties.rationale: On validation failure, loops back to Summarization node (not START or Inquiry) for blind re-generation. Max 5 retries with temperature=0.7 as only variation source. Routes to UnhandledNode when exhausted.

properties.name: validation_router
properties.rationale: Conditional edge function routing on pass_validation and retry_count. Pass -> END, fail with retries -> Summarization, fail exhausted -> UnhandledNode.

properties.name: Retry Counter Lifecycle
properties.rationale: retry_count field on WorkflowState reset to 0 by IntentCheckNode for each new user message, incremented on validation failure, unchanged on pass.

properties.name: PromptRegistry Package
properties.rationale: Modular registry package (muggle/registry/) with __init__.py, model.py, prompt.py. PromptRegistry provides typed accessors (get_system_prompt, get_user_prompt) looking in system/ and user/ subfolders.

properties.name: Custom Exception Hierarchy
properties.rationale: MuggleError base class with PromptNotFoundError subclass. Exceptions include descriptive messages with failed operation context (prompt name and type). Defined in leaf-level muggle/exceptions.py to avoid circular imports.

properties.name: Codebase Reorganization Change
properties.rationale: BREAKING: move all source to src/muggle/, reorganize into core/infra/app/shared layers, relocate prompts to package data with importlib.resources, update pyproject.toml, IDE configs, and docs.

properties.name: Q&A Chatbot v1 Change
properties.rationale: Implement SPA chat interface, Flask /chat API, modular ChatProcessor with DeepSeek via LangChain. Foundation for future LangGraph integration.

properties.name: Response Validation Change
properties.rationale: Add ValidateNode scoring Inquiry output, insert after InquiryNode with conditional routing, add retry_count and pass_validation to WorkflowState, add [validate] config section.

properties.name: Prompt Registry Refinement Change
properties.rationale: Split registry.py into package, introduce exceptions.py with MuggleError and PromptNotFoundError, integrate PromptRegistry into ChatProcessor for template-driven responses.

properties.name: response-validation capability
properties.rationale: LLM-based quality gate scoring InquiryNode output, loop back to Summarization on failure (up to 5 retries), route to UnhandledNode when exhausted. Threshold configurable via config.toml [validate] section.

properties.name: error-handling capability
properties.rationale: Domain-specific exceptions (MuggleError, PromptNotFoundError) with descriptive messages including failed operation and context.

properties.name: prompt-registry capability
properties.rationale: Typed prompt accessors (get_system_prompt, get_user_prompt) looking in system/ and user/ subfolders. Raises PromptNotFoundError on missing prompts.

properties.name: Internal Prompt Discovery
properties.rationale: PromptRegistry must discover and load prompts from within package structure using importlib.resources, defaulting to muggle.infra.prompts package.

properties.title: AI Integration Spec - PromptRegistry Refinement
properties.description: Spec requiring ChatProcessor to use PromptRegistry for template retrieval and fail-safe handling for missing prompts

properties.title: Chat API Spec (Added)

properties.title: AI Processor Spec (Removed)

properties.title: Unify Pydantic V2 Style - Metadata
properties.description: Schema: spec-driven, created: 2026-05-03

properties.title: Unify Pydantic V2 Style - Tasks

properties.title: Unify Pydantic V2 Style - Design
properties.description: Adopt unified Pydantic V2 style (Annotated[xxx] = Field(xxx)) for clarity and LangGraph state management compatibility
properties.rationale: Resolves ambiguity with LangGraph introspection; follows Pydantic V2 recommended style

properties.title: Unify Pydantic V2 Style - Proposal
properties.description: Refactor all Pydantic models in graph_processor.py to consistent Annotated + Field style; code style refactoring only

properties.title: General Spec (Pydantic V2 Style)
properties.description: Empty spec file

properties.title: User Prompt Template - Default
properties.description: Simple user message template with {{ message }} variable

properties.title: System Prompt - CRISPE Example
properties.description: CRISPE framework prompt template: Capacity, Insight, Statement, Personality, Experiment sections with {{ task_description }}

properties.title: System Prompt - Inquiry
properties.description: Senior Insurance Underwriter persona for generating comprehensive insurance responses with Condition-Benefit mapping, claims checklists, and markdown comparisons

properties.title: System Prompt - Strict Intent Check (2026-05-03)
properties.description: Binary classification prompt: returns exactly 'insurance' or 'unknown' with Insurance Domain Weight calculation

properties.title: System Prompt - Context-Aware Intent Check
properties.description: Context-aware intent validator: returns True for greetings/context/insurance queries, False only for adversarial jailbreak attempts

properties.title: System Prompt - Default
properties.description: Simple generic prompt: 'You are a helpful assistant.'

properties.title: System Prompt - Query Rewrite
properties.description: Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval (CLIR) prompt generating dual en-US and zh-CN search queries optimized for insurance terminology

properties.title: System Prompt - Validation
properties.description: Insurance Compliance Auditor persona performing weighted scoring (Compliance 0.5, Accuracy 0.3, Resolution 0.2) against configurable threshold

properties.name: Registry-Driven Chat Processing
properties.description: ChatProcessor must retrieve and apply prompt templates from PromptRegistry before invoking LLM
properties.rationale: Decouples prompt management from processor logic; enables dynamic template loading

properties.name: Fail-Safe Missing Prompt Handling
properties.description: ChatProcessor must gracefully handle PromptNotFoundError by logging and returning user-friendly error message
properties.rationale: Prevents silent failures and provides clear feedback when AI configuration is incomplete

properties.name: AI to LLM Rename Initiative
properties.description: Systematic rename of AI terminology to LLM/Model across core, infra, API, frontend, config, docs, and specs
properties.rationale: AI is overly broad; LLM provides better technical precision aligned with LangChain/LangGraph implementation

properties.name: Config Key Rename [ai] to [llm]
properties.description: Rename config.toml [ai] section to [llm], rename ai_config variable to llm_config
properties.rationale: More precise terminology; breaking change but aligns with modern naming

properties.name: AI-to-LLM Capability Consolidation
properties.description: Remove ai-processor spec and consolidate all requirements into llm-processor as single source of truth
properties.rationale: Eliminates redundancy between ai-processor and llm-processor specifications

properties.name: LLM Processor
properties.description: Primary processing capability for message handling using LLM, using PromptRegistry for prompt templates

properties.name: AI Processor (Deprecated)
properties.description: Former processor capability, now removed and consolidated into LLM Processor

properties.name: Model Configuration (TOML)
properties.description: TOML-based configuration utility loading LLM settings (provider, model name, temperature) under [llm] section for init_chat_model

properties.name: Frontend LLM Class Rename
properties.description: Rename CSS class .ai to .llm, update JavaScript message types from ai to llm, update backend jsonify calls

properties.name: Pydantic V2 Unified Annotation Style
properties.description: Adopt xxx: Annotated[xxx] = Field(xxx) consistently across all Pydantic models
properties.rationale: Pydantic V2 recommended style; resolves ambiguity with LangGraph state management introspection

properties.name: LangGraph State Compatibility
properties.description: Ensuring Pydantic model annotations are compatible with LangGraph's state management and introspection mechanisms

properties.name: CRISPE Prompt Framework
properties.description: Prompt structure: Capacity, Insight, Statement, Personality, Experiment — used in the example system prompt template

properties.name: Insurance Domain Taxonomy
properties.description: Domain-specific terminology: lines of authority (Life, Health, Property), contractual elements (Premiums, Deductibles, Riders), processes (Underwriting, Claims), legal terms (Beneficiary, Annuity, COI)

properties.name: Intent Check Classification
properties.description: Determines if user message is in-scope (insurance-related) for the RAG chatbot; has two variants: strict binary and context-aware

properties.name: Strict Binary Intent Check
properties.description: Outputs exactly 'insurance' or 'unknown' using Insurance Domain Weight calculation; no conversational filler

properties.name: Context-Aware Intent Validator
properties.description: Returns True for greetings, personal context, insurance queries; False only for adversarial jailbreak attempts

properties.name: Cross-Lingual Query Rewrite
properties.description: Generates dual standalone queries (en-US + zh-CN) optimized for insurance terminology in respective languages

properties.name: Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval (CLIR)
properties.description: Maps insurance-specific intent across English and Simplified Chinese linguistic spaces for multi-lingual k-NN search optimization

properties.name: Inquiry Response Generation
properties.description: Senior Insurance Underwriter persona generating comprehensive responses with Condition-Benefit mapping, claims checklists, comparison tables

properties.name: Validation Quality Gate
properties.description: Weighted scoring (Compliance 0.5, Accuracy 0.3, Resolution 0.2) with configurable threshold for auto-approval or rejection

properties.name: ChatProcessor
properties.description: Core processor receiving user messages, invoking PromptRegistry for prompts, and sending message lists to LLM

nodes.label: Chat Interface
nodes.rationale: Single-page chat UI with minimal server interaction; all state is ephemeral (sessionStorage) or server-managed (thread_id). No client-side routing or component framework.

nodes.label: Chat Container
nodes.rationale: Flexbox column layout with overflow-y: auto to handle message scrolling. Newest messages appear at bottom via scrollTop = scrollHeight.

nodes.label: Input Area
nodes.rationale: Fixed bottom bar with flexbox gap layout. Sticky positioning via parent body flex layout rather than position: fixed.

nodes.label: sendMessage Function
nodes.rationale: Async function that orchestrates the full send cycle: read input, disable UI, POST to /chat, handle response/error, re-enable UI. No streaming support — waits for full JSON response.

nodes.label: /chat POST Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Single API contract: accepts {message: string, thread_id: string|null}, returns {response: string, thread_id: string|null, error: string|null}. No streaming, no SSE.

nodes.label: Thread Persistence via sessionStorage
nodes.rationale: sessionStorage ties thread lifetime to browser tab lifespan — thread_id is lost on tab close. No localStorage fallback, no multi-tab coordination.

nodes.label: Disabled-UX During Request Pattern
nodes.rationale: Both input and button disabled during fetch to prevent duplicate submissions. Re-enabled in finally block ensures recovery even on network error.

nodes.label: Chat Bubble CSS Styling
nodes.rationale: User bubbles right-aligned (blue), LLM bubbles left-aligned (gray). max-width 70% keeps bubbles from spanning full width. No avatars, timestamps, or markdown rendering.

links.detail: Law collection change reuses BM25 schema, hybrid search API, and RRF fusion infrastructure established by the BM25 change
Both modify the retrieval/inference pipeline but target different layers (search algorithm vs processor orchestration)
Moves GraphProcessor from experimental to core package
Moves ProcessorInterface to core/__init__.py for discoverability
Memory fix applies to the already-promoted GraphProcessor in core package
Fixes Field(default_factory=list) placement outside Annotated block in WorkflowState
Generates both header_vector from chapter-prefixed heading and content_vector from combined heading and body
Both are markdown-based document loaders that parse structured content and ingest into VectorStoreManager with dual embeddings (header_vector + content_vector) and BM25 sparse vectors
GraphProcessor accepts list of VectorStoreManager instances for retrieval pipeline
Moves from two independent dense search calls to one hybrid_search call
DashScopeRerank remains as cross-encoder refinement after hybrid recall
Loops over list of VectorStoreManager instances searching each with lang_tag filter
Searches zh-CN content with zh-CN query and en-US content with en-US query, then merges and deduplicates by id
Law collection uses the identical schema as aia_faq including BM25 sparse vector field
Combines content_vector, header_vector, and sparse_vector in a single Milvus hybrid search call
QueryRewriteNode returns vector_store_queries dict with zh-CN and en-US keys
Retrieves system prompts for internal graph nodes via PromptRegistry

properties.reason: Both patterns manage component dependencies: ModelRegistry centralizes model lifecycle, dependency injection provides VectorStoreManager to GraphProcessor
Health check endpoint created in first change, then simplified in later change
Graceful warmup introduced in first change, then removed as redundant in later change
Both changes modify GraphProcessor nodes: one adds RAG nodes, the other refactors node execution from agent to structured output
